name: Chickpea Snacks
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all seasoning ingredients in a small bowl.
2. Mix 1 tablespoon of the seasoned mix with the artificial sweetener and set aside.
3. Place the chickpeas and water in a large container, add the remaining seasoned mix, and soak overnight in the refrigerator.
4. The next day, place the chickpeas and soaking water in a 5-quart pot over high heat. Add apple juice and cook for 20 minutes.
5. Add vegetable stock and continue cooking until the peas are tender (about 40 minutes). Remove from heat and drain the peas in a colander.
6. Rinse the drained peas well and return them to the container.
7. Sprinkle the peas evenly with the reserved sweetened seasoning mix.
8. Cook the peas one of two ways:
a. Use a food dehydrator and dry until crunchy (about 20-22 hours).
b. Use an oven set at 150°F and dry until crunchy (about 20-22 hours), stirring or rotating the pans every couple of hours for even drying.
Comments:
- If white pepper is unavailable, use black pepper (1 teaspoon total).
- The recipe works better with artificial sweetener than regular sugar.
- Without a dehydrator, cook in an oven at 150°F. Adjust cooking time based on door openings and pan movements.
